source: policy changes in the mortgage market

source: a tightening of credit conditions or a deterioration in investor risk sentiment
target: corporates in such funding markets more exposed

metadata.original_sentence: In markets serving higher-risk borrowers, such as leveraged loans and private credit, a larger proportion of outstanding debt has shorter maturity and therefore requires refinancing over the next few years, leaving corporates in such funding markets more exposed to a tightening of credit conditions or a deterioration in investor risk sentiment.

target: the aggregate Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital ratio starts at 14.5% and falls to a low point of 11.0%

source: changes to an economy’s supply capacity
target: GDP growth

metadata.original_sentence: In the long term, GDP growth is driven by changes to an economy’s supply capacity, which is determined by the amount of labour and capital available, and the efficiency with which these factors can be combined – known as total factor productivity.

source: diffusion of information and communications technologies
target: total factor productivity growth

metadata.original_sentence: In the 1990s, total factor productivity growth in most western economies was high, driven by the diffusion of information and communications technologies such as the internet, which have the characteristics of a General-Purpose Technology (GPT).

target: expansion beyond traditional business lending

target: increased vulnerabilities

target: reduction in the reliance of SMEs on bank financing

metadata.original_sentence: The aftermath of the recession saw a reduction in the reliance of SMEs on bank financing, with the proportion of small businesses seeking external finance falling from 65% between 1987–90, to around 40% between 1995–97.

target: house prices fell

metadata.original_sentence: Household debt was stable throughout much of the 1990s between 60%–65% of GDP, reflecting the subdued housing market that followed the 1990s recession as rates rose and house prices fell (Chart A).

target: over-leveraged bank and private sector balance sheets

metadata.original_sentence: Household and corporate borrowing accelerated in the lead-up to the 2008–09 global financial crisis (GFC) resulting in over-leveraged bank and private sector balance sheets.

target: rapid house price growth

metadata.original_sentence: Household debt-to-GDP grew by around 40 percentage points from the late 1990’s to 2008 and peaked at over 100% of GDP, mirroring rapid house price growth partly driven by increased competition among mortgage lenders (Chart A).

target: strengthened the resilience

metadata.original_sentence: Macroprudential policies, which strengthened the resilience and reduced excessive risk-taking across the financial system, were introduced after the GFC to reduce this procyclical behaviour.

target: reduced excessive risk-taking

metadata.original_sentence: The flat total household debt stock meant it declined relative to GDP, falling to below 90% in 2014.

target: bank lending and the stock of household and corporate credit rose

target: strengthened and increased confidence in the banking system

metadata.original_sentence: This followed the implementation of post-crisis regulatory reforms, including higher capital and liquidity requirements that strengthened and increased confidence in the banking system.

target: marked reduction in household and corporate debt-to- GDP ratios

metadata.original_sentence: The economy was hit by a series of shocks starting in 2020 that ultimately resulted in high inflation driving a marked reduction in household and corporate debt-to- GDP ratios, while post-GFC regulation has enabled the banking system to weather shocks while supporting the economy.

target: severe cash flow issues

metadata.original_sentence: The stock of corporate debt rose by 6% over 2020 H1 as many businesses, especially SMEs, faced severe cash flow issues due to lockdowns and reduced consumer demand.

target: surge in demand for emergency finance

metadata.original_sentence: This led to a surge in demand for emergency finance, including government-backed loans.

target: large increase in household and corporate debt-to-GDP ratios

metadata.original_sentence: This, combined with a roughly 16% contraction in nominal GDP during lockdown, resulted in large increase in household and corporate debt-to-GDP ratios.

target: increased inflation and nominal GDP

target: improve their funding ratios

metadata.original_sentence: Historically, pension funds have been large buyers of longer-term government bonds, but have played less of a role in recent years as higher interest rates improve their funding ratios, and an ageing population may encourage funds to shift toward shorter-term assets to ensure liquidity for paying current benefits.

source: progress in AI systems and the development of physical infrastructure
target: companies across multiple sectors ... being at least partly dependent on AI progress for their current and expected future earnings

metadata.original_sentence: The progress in AI systems and the development of physical infrastructure to train, use and power them has resulted in a wide range of companies across multiple sectors – from technology to utilities and capital goods (referred to as ‘AI companies’ in this box) – being at least partly dependent on AI progress for their current and expected future earnings.

target: share prices of many AI companies ... appearing historically expensive

metadata.original_sentence: The share prices of many AI companies are partly underpinned by high expected future earnings growth over several years, contributing to those companies – and subsequently the equity indices which they comprise a significant part of – appearing historically expensive in valuation metrics which consider past, current or only near-term future earnings (Chart A).

source: issued a large amount of debt this year to finance AI infrastructure spending
target: five-year credit default swap spreads of Oracle ... has widened

metadata.original_sentence: For example, the five-year credit default swap spreads of Oracle – an AI company which has lower free cash flow margins than some other larger hyperscalers and has issued a large amount of debt this year to finance AI infrastructure spending – has widened from less than 40 basis points to around 120 basis points since end-July (by contrast, the credit default swap spreads of US IG corporates more broadly – as proxied by the CDX North American IG five-year index – are broadly unchanged over the same period).

source: tracing the final location of credit risk is challenging
target: complicating assessments of who ultimately bears losses if conditions deteriorate

metadata.original_sentence: As a result, tracing the final location of credit risk is challenging, complicating assessments of who ultimately bears losses if conditions deteriorate.

target: risks to systemic financial institutions

metadata.original_sentence: However, the collapse of Archegos Capital Management in March 2021, after failing to meet margin calls on their equity total return swap positions, demonstrated how leveraged equity positions can result in risks to systemic financial institutions through prime brokerage exposures when prices fall.

target: increase in US real consumption

metadata.original_sentence: On top of the contribution of AI investment to US GDP growth, Bank staff estimate that increases in US equity prices have contributed to around 10% of the increase in US real consumption since the start of 2024, with AI-related equities the main driver during this period.

target: direct wealth effects on consumer spending

metadata.original_sentence: The direct wealth effects on consumer spending in the UK of an AI-driven fall in equity prices are likely to be comparatively lower than the US due to the significantly smaller role of direct equity holdings as a share of household wealth.

target: direct impact would become larger

metadata.original_sentence: While at present the degree of impact on credit markets of a re-evaluation of future AI company earnings would likely be modest, if debt issuance increased significantly, the direct impact would become larger.

target: large margin calls

metadata.original_sentence: Very large shocks to commodity prices can have spillover consequences for systemic institutions, as seen in 2022 when large margin calls in LME Nickel futures markets following unprecedented increases in prices forced a suspension of trading by the central counterparty (CCP) LME Clear.

target: forced a suspension of trading

target: quoted mortgage rates have continued to decrease

metadata.original_sentence: In line with the latest developments in monetary policy and a competitive mortgage market, quoted mortgage rates have continued to decrease.

source: rates for private non- financial corporates
target: declining 14 basis points

metadata.original_sentence: Effective rates on new bank lending to corporates have also decreased, with rates for private non- financial corporates declining 14 basis points from August to September.

source: 300 basis points increase in mortgage spreads
target: passes through to mortgage borrowers

metadata.original_sentence: It assumes both a cumulative 5% fall in disposable (post-tax) household incomes by the end of 2027 – a little larger than in the GFC – as well as a 300 basis points increase in mortgage spreads, which passes through to mortgage borrowers with a lag.

target: Borrowing costs have decreased

metadata.original_sentence: Borrowing costs have decreased following recent reductions in Bank Rate.

target: increase in mortgage repayments

metadata.original_sentence: However, some households are expected to see an increase in mortgage repayments, as the full impact of higher interest rates has not yet passed through to all mortgagors.

target: monthly mortgage repayments are projected to increase

metadata.original_sentence: On balance, for the typical owner-occupier mortgagor rolling off a fixed rate in the next two years, their monthly mortgage repayments are projected to increase by £64 (8%), with some households facing much larger increases.

target: insurance against a marked and unsustainable loosening in underwriting standards

metadata.original_sentence: This measure provides insurance against a marked and unsustainable loosening in underwriting standards and a further significant increase in the number of very highly indebted households.

target: insurance against ... a further significant increase in the number of very highly indebted households

metadata.original_sentence: This measure provides insurance against a marked and unsustainable loosening in underwriting standards and a further significant increase in the number of very highly indebted households.

target: fall in median stress rates

metadata.original_sentence: However, several major lenders have already changed their behaviour in response to the MCOB statement, in part driving a fall in median stress rates on Q3 completions of around 100 basis points since Q1.

source: end of the stamp duty land tax holiday
target: depressed

metadata.original_sentence: The share of high LTI lending increased to 9.5% in Q3, in line with Q1 and up from 7.6% in Q2, which was depressed by the end of the stamp duty land tax holiday.

target: additional borrowers to enter the market

metadata.original_sentence: While looser credit conditions allow additional borrowers to enter the market in the near term, staff analysis suggests that, unless there is also an increase in housing supply, increased credit availability has limited ability directly to address barriers to home ownership.

target: corporate net debt to earnings ratio ticked up

metadata.original_sentence: The corporate net debt to earnings ratio ticked up slightly in 2025 Q2 as net debt increased and profits fell slightly.

target: corporate net debt to earnings ratio ticked up

metadata.original_sentence: The corporate net debt to earnings ratio ticked up slightly in 2025 Q2 as net debt increased and profits fell slightly.

source: aggregate ratio remains well below Covid (166%) and post-GFC (230%) highs
target: reducing the risk that indebted corporates would materially amplify a shock

metadata.original_sentence: At 134%, the aggregate ratio remains well below Covid (166%) and post-GFC (230%) highs, reducing the risk that indebted corporates would materially amplify a shock relative to the past.

target: Measures of corporate vulnerability would remain low

source: small firms with limited debt and employment
target: most insolvencies are still driven by

metadata.original_sentence: However, there has been an uptick in insolvencies for medium-sized corporates, although most insolvencies are still driven by small firms with limited debt and employment.

target: more exposed

metadata.original_sentence: The share of SMEs in arrears is highest in the accommodation and food, construction, and retail trade sectors, which are more exposed to increased input costs and increased labour market costs associated with a higher minimum wage and the increase in employer’s national insurance contributions (NICs).

target: more exposed

target: more exposed

target: limited implications for banking system losses

metadata.original_sentence: However, SME lending has limited implications for banking system losses in aggregate because their bank exposures are relatively small, and a portion of this lending is government guaranteed.

source: a portion of this lending is government guaranteed
target: limited implications for banking system losses

target: tighter financing conditions

metadata.original_sentence: Some highly leveraged corporate borrowers relying on market-based finance areparticularly exposed to tighter financing conditions, which could be triggered by global shocks.

target: particularly exposed

metadata.original_sentence: Some highly leveraged corporate borrowers relying on market-based finance areparticularly exposed to tighter financing conditions, which could be triggered by global shocks.

target: refinancing challenges

metadata.original_sentence: UK corporates borrowing via market-based finance, such as leveraged loans, high-yield bonds and private credit, might face refinancing challenges due to higher interest rates and greater borrowing levels.

target: refinancing challenges

metadata.original_sentence: UK corporates borrowing via market-based finance, such as leveraged loans, high-yield bonds and private credit, might face refinancing challenges due to higher interest rates and greater borrowing levels.

target: benefitted corporates on floating interest rates

metadata.original_sentence: While interest rates remain high, cuts in Bank Rate will have benefitted corporates on floating interest rates (eg variable-rate bank loans) that decreased in line with Bank Rate.

target: interest rate increases

metadata.original_sentence: Corporate bonds, which comprise a large part of total market-based financing in the UK, are fixed for 10 years on average, meaning issuers are likely to face interest rate increases as they refinance.

target: increase the exposure of these corporates to higher interest rates

metadata.original_sentence: The steeper refinancing walls increase the exposure of these corporates to higher interest rates and deteriorations in investor risk sentiment.

target: deteriorations in investor risk sentiment

metadata.original_sentence: The steeper refinancing walls increase the exposure of these corporates to higher interest rates and deteriorations in investor risk sentiment.

target: Credit spreads across a range of markets widened

metadata.original_sentence: Credit spreads across a range of markets widened marginally in November compared to the previous month, partly as a response to the recent high-profile US corporate defaults, but they remain very low by historical standards (Section 2).

target: reduced corporate borrowing costs

metadata.original_sentence: This has reduced corporate borrowing costs, encouraging higher levels of corporate issuance and easing refinancing burdens.

target: encouraging higher levels of corporate issuance

metadata.original_sentence: This has reduced corporate borrowing costs, encouraging higher levels of corporate issuance and easing refinancing burdens.

target: easing refinancing burdens

metadata.original_sentence: This has reduced corporate borrowing costs, encouraging higher levels of corporate issuance and easing refinancing burdens.

target: pose some borrower and lender resilience challenges

metadata.original_sentence: Because firms using riskier forms of market-based finance comprise at least 12% of UK employment, stress in these markets could pose some borrower and lender resilience challenges.

source: a decline in their cost of equity
target: UK bank valuations

metadata.original_sentence: UK bank valuations are near post-GFC highs, supported by a decline in their cost of equity and an increase in expected RoTE.

target: UK bank valuations

metadata.original_sentence: UK bank valuations are near post-GFC highs, supported by a decline in their cost of equity and an increase in expected RoTE.

source: an increase in expected aggregate RoTE
target: Valuations of major UK banks

metadata.original_sentence: Valuations of major UK banks have increased due to both an increase in expected aggregate RoTE and a decrease in their cost of equity in recent years.

source: a decrease in their cost of equity
target: Valuations of major UK banks

metadata.original_sentence: Valuations of major UK banks have increased due to both an increase in expected aggregate RoTE and a decrease in their cost of equity in recent years.

target: gilt repo rates rose temporarily

metadata.original_sentence: As expected, gilt repo rates rose temporarily at the end of October, in part due to the reduction in reserves, but retraced following usage of the Bank’s facilities.

target: gilt repo rates

metadata.original_sentence: As expected, gilt repo rates rose temporarily at the end of October, in part due to the reduction in reserves, but retraced following usage of the Bank’s facilities.

source: buyers bringing mortgage purchases forward to Q1 ahead of the stamp duty changes
target: decline in 2025 Q2

metadata.original_sentence: Following a decline in 2025 Q2 that reflected the impact of buyers bringing mortgage purchases forward to Q1 ahead of the stamp duty changes, mortgage activity picked up in Q3, including the proportion of new loans at higher LTI and LTV ratios.

target: mortgage activity picked up

metadata.original_sentence: This was driven by stronger-than-expected demand and an easing in lending conditions.

target: mortgage activity picked up

metadata.original_sentence: This was driven by stronger-than-expected demand and an easing in lending conditions.

source: FCA’s clarification of its Mortgage Conduct of Business (MCOB) rules
target: banks reducing stress rates on mortgage lending

metadata.original_sentence: Some of this easing reflected banks reducing stress rates on mortgage lending in response to the FCA’s clarification of its Mortgage Conduct of Business (MCOB) rules (Section 3).

source: full impact of the FCA clarification
target: further increases in lending at higher LTV and LTI ratios

metadata.original_sentence: Looking forward, there may be further increases in lending at higher LTV and LTI ratios as the full impact of the FCA clarification feeds through, along with effect of the adjustment made by the PRA to the application of the LTI flow limit, following the FPC’s updated recommendation in Q2 (Section 3).

source: adjustment made by the PRA to the application of the LTI flow limit
target: further increases in lending at higher LTV and LTI ratios

metadata.original_sentence: Looking forward, there may be further increases in lending at higher LTV and LTI ratios as the full impact of the FCA clarification feeds through, along with effect of the adjustment made by the PRA to the application of the LTI flow limit, following the FPC’s updated recommendation in Q2 (Section 3).

target: compressed spreads

metadata.original_sentence: Increased competition is said to be contributing to compressed spreads and an easing in some loan terms.

target: an easing in some loan terms

metadata.original_sentence: Increased competition is said to be contributing to compressed spreads and an easing in some loan terms.

source: mortgage activity brought forward to the first quarter
target: A fall in gross lending volumes

metadata.original_sentence: Chart 4.2: A fall in gross lending volumes from banks and building societies in 2025 Q2 reflected the impact of mortgage activity brought forward to the first quarter.

target: banks’ intraday exposures, through their PTF clients, exceeding end-of day positions

metadata.original_sentence: As PTF activities often involve high volumes of intraday trading, this can result in banks’ intraday exposures, through their PTF clients, exceeding end-of day positions.

target: pushing up on borrowing costs

metadata.original_sentence: This assessment weighs the macroeconomic costs of capital, which stem from the impact of higher capital pushing up on borrowing costs, against the benefits of capital, which come about because higher bank capital reduces the likelihood and costs of financial crises.

target: reduces the likelihood and costs of financial crises

metadata.original_sentence: This assessment weighs the macroeconomic costs of capital, which stem from the impact of higher capital pushing up on borrowing costs, against the benefits of capital, which come about because higher bank capital reduces the likelihood and costs of financial crises.

source: fall in banks’ average risk weights
target: £60 billion less nominal capital

metadata.original_sentence: The 7½ percentage point fall in banks’ average risk weights (measured excluding central bank reserves) since the beginning of 2016 means that the FPC’s previous Tier 1 benchmark is now associated with around £60 billion less nominal capital, based on the size of current balance sheets, than would have been the case absent the fall in risk weights; systemic buffers are lower than envisaged in 2015 as some banks have decreased in systemic importance; and the implementation of Basel 3.1 on 1 January 2027 will improve risk measurement, allowing the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) to reduce Pillar 2A minimum requirements by around ½ percentage point.

target: reduce Pillar 2A minimum requirements

metadata.original_sentence: The 7½ percentage point fall in banks’ average risk weights (measured excluding central bank reserves) since the beginning of 2016 means that the FPC’s previous Tier 1 benchmark is now associated with around £60 billion less nominal capital, based on the size of current balance sheets, than would have been the case absent the fall in risk weights; systemic buffers are lower than envisaged in 2015 as some banks have decreased in systemic importance; and the implementation of Basel 3.1 on 1 January 2027 will improve risk measurement, allowing the Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) to reduce Pillar 2A minimum requirements by around ½ percentage point.

target: level of system-wide Tier 1 capital requirements is expected to fall

metadata.original_sentence: As a result, the level of system-wide Tier 1 capital requirements is expected to fall to around 13% of RWAs when Basel 3.1 is implemented, consistent with the FPC’s updated benchmark.

target: credit conditions for households and businesses improved

metadata.original_sentence: As the economic outlook improved following recent shocks, credit conditions for households and businesses improved commensurately and have since evolved in line with the macroeconomic outlook.

target: material increase in UK banks’ equity market valuations

metadata.original_sentence: Higher returns on tangible equity (RoTE) – and investors’ increasing confidence that those returns can be sustained – have supported a material increase in UK banks’ equity market valuations (Section 4).

target: material increase in UK banks’ equity market valuations

metadata.original_sentence: Higher returns on tangible equity (RoTE) – and investors’ increasing confidence that those returns can be sustained – have supported a material increase in UK banks’ equity market valuations (Section 4).

source: premium investors demand to hold UK banks’ equity relative to their debt has fallen
target: reduces the cost to banks of increasing their share of equity funding

metadata.original_sentence: Relatedly, there is evidence that the premium investors demand to hold UK banks’ equity relative to their debt has fallen, which reduces the cost to banks of increasing their share of equity funding relative to debt funding, all else equal.

target: significant reductions in long-run expected GDP

metadata.original_sentence: Analysis suggests that materially lower capital requirements could lead to significant reductions in long-run expected GDP.

source: credible and effective resolution arrangements, effective supervision, structural reform such as the implementation of ring-fencing, and the Committee’s active use of the time-varying CCyB
target: materially reduced the FPC’s assessment of the appropriate level of capital

metadata.original_sentence: Those judgements were related to credible and effective resolution arrangements, effective supervision, structural reform such as the implementation of ring-fencing, and the Committee’s active use of the time-varying CCyB. Together, these judgements materially reduced the FPC’s assessment of the appropriate level of capital in 2015.

source: reducing system-wide capital requirements materially below the FPC’s updated benchmark of 13%
target: significant reductions in long-run expected GDP

metadata.original_sentence: Analysis also suggests that reducing system-wide capital requirements materially below the FPC’s updated benchmark of 13% (unless due to further improvements in risk measurement that allow overlaps to be removed from Pillar 2A requirements) could be associated with significant reductions in long-run expected GDP through the costs of greater instability – especially if those reductions in capital were to undermine the credibility of the resolution regime as a result of lower overall loss-absorbing capacity.

target: banks to support a material increase in lending

metadata.original_sentence: But to the extent that incentives to maintain excess capital can be reduced by enhancing buffer usability, this could allow banks to support a material increase in lending.

source: reduction of the FPC’s important Tier 1 benchmark to 13%
target: provide banks with greater certainty and confidence to use their existing capital to support lending to the real economy

metadata.original_sentence: The reduction of the FPC’s important Tier 1 benchmark to 13% should also provide banks with greater certainty and confidence to use their existing capital to support lending to the real economy.

source: underlying reductions in the riskiness of banks’ exposures
target: leverage ratio has become more binding

metadata.original_sentence: For example, the FPC will explore the extent to which the leverage ratio has become more binding as a result of underlying reductions in the riskiness of banks’ exposures.

target: severe supply chain disruptions

metadata.original_sentence: Global trading relationships fragment, leading to severe supply chain disruptions, which contributes to a large fall in world trade volumes.

target: large fall in world trade volumes

metadata.original_sentence: Global trading relationships fragment, leading to severe supply chain disruptions, which contributes to a large fall in world trade volumes.

target: large falls in asset prices

metadata.original_sentence: Increased risk aversion leads to large falls in asset prices, while elevated sovereign debt levels in advanced economies put further upward pressure on government bond yields.

source: elevated sovereign debt levels in advanced economies
target: upward pressure on government bond yields

metadata.original_sentence: Increased risk aversion leads to large falls in asset prices, while elevated sovereign debt levels in advanced economies put further upward pressure on government bond yields.

source: lower credit demand and a worsening economic outlook
target: Credit growth slows

metadata.original_sentence: Credit growth slows in the early part of the stress reflecting lower credit demand and a worsening economic outlook, however it recovers in the later years and lending to the UK real economy is assumed to increase by more than 6.5% over the scenario horizon.

target: aggregate CET1 capital ratio falls

metadata.original_sentence: The aggregate CET1 capital ratio falls to a low point of 11.0% in the first year of the stress, which equates to an aggregate capital drawdown of 3.5 percentage points.

target: capital drawdown

metadata.original_sentence: The key drivers of the capital drawdown are an increase in credit impairments (Section 5.3) and traded risk losses (Section 5.4), which are only partially offset by higher NII in the first year of the stress (Section 5.5).

target: capital drawdown

metadata.original_sentence: The key drivers of the capital drawdown are an increase in credit impairments (Section 5.3) and traded risk losses (Section 5.4), which are only partially offset by higher NII in the first year of the stress (Section 5.5).

target: capital drawdown

metadata.original_sentence: The key drivers of the capital drawdown are an increase in credit impairments (Section 5.3) and traded risk losses (Section 5.4), which are only partially offset by higher NII in the first year of the stress (Section 5.5).

target: reduce the aggregate CET1 capital ratio

metadata.original_sentence: Credit impairments reduce the aggregate CET1 capital ratio by 3.3 percentage points at the low point (year 1).

target: reduced the affordability impact

metadata.original_sentence: The less severe inflation path in this test has also reduced the affordability impact.

source: improved asset quality in non-UK consumer lending
target: Non-UK consumer credit impairments are £19 billion, which is lower than in the 2022/23 ACS

metadata.original_sentence: This is due to improved asset quality in non-UK consumer lending which includes a range of countries and products.

source: rising costs, trade disruption and higher interest rates
target: impairments

metadata.original_sentence: There are common drivers of impairments across corporate portfolios – for example 70% of banks reported impairments were from sectors judged vulnerable to rising costs, trade disruption and higher interest rates.

source: price falls over the previous 18 months
target: increased LTV ratios

metadata.original_sentence: UK CRE impairment rates are broadly unchanged since the 2022/23 ACS, as price falls over the previous 18 months have contributed to increased LTV ratios.

source: reduction in CRE losses in Hong Kong and China
target: This is significantly lower than in the 2022/23 ACS

metadata.original_sentence: This is significantly lower than in the 2022/23 ACS, primarily driven by a reduction in CRE losses in Hong Kong and China.

source: increasing the UK house price fall beyond 30%
target: increasing marginal impact on bank losses

metadata.original_sentence: Chart 5.2 shows that increasing the UK house price fall beyond 30% would tend to have an increasing marginal impact on bank losses as a greater proportion of their lending would be secured on collateral worth less than the value of the loan.

source: the fall in UK house prices were 10 percentage points greater
target: aggregate UK mortgage impairments would be around twice as great

metadata.original_sentence: For instance, if the fall in UK house prices were 10 percentage points greater, aggregate UK mortgage impairments would be around twice as great as in the Bank Capital Stress Test.

target: reduces banks’ aggregate CET1 capital ratio by 0.8 percentage points

metadata.original_sentence: 5.4: Traded risk The traded risk scenario reduces banks’ aggregate CET1 capital ratio by 0.8 percentage points in the first year of the stress.

target: reduces banks’ capital by around £15 billion

metadata.original_sentence: Overall, the net impact of these factors reduces banks’ capital by around £15 billion, equivalent to 0.8 percentage points of the CET1 capital ratio.

source: a sharp fall in fees from advisory activities
target: Revenues decline

metadata.original_sentence: Revenues decline in the first year of the stress, driven by a sharp fall in fees from advisory activities, though they are somewhat supported by resilient client trading activities in response to increased financial market volatility.

target: resilient client trading activities

metadata.original_sentence: Revenues decline in the first year of the stress, driven by a sharp fall in fees from advisory activities, though they are somewhat supported by resilient client trading activities in response to increased financial market volatility.

target: losses in fair-value through other comprehensive income securities, trading inventories and related valuations adjustments

metadata.original_sentence: However, this revenue is offset by £35 billion of losses due to the stress scenario leading to losses in fair-value through other comprehensive income securities, trading inventories and related valuations adjustments.

source: Higher interest rates and a higher spread between government bond yields and OIS rates
target: lower fair value of bond holdings

metadata.original_sentence: Higher interest rates and a higher spread between government bond yields and OIS rates in the scenario lead to lower fair value of bond holdings in banks’ liquid asset buffers, which contributed to a £15.5 billion reduction in capital.

source: lower fair value of bond holdings
target: £15.5 billion reduction in capital

metadata.original_sentence: Higher interest rates and a higher spread between government bond yields and OIS rates in the scenario lead to lower fair value of bond holdings in banks’ liquid asset buffers, which contributed to a £15.5 billion reduction in capital.

source: Trading book losses from market risk shocks, defaults of selected vulnerable counterparties and valuation adjustments
target: £19 billion reduction in capital

metadata.original_sentence: Trading book losses from market risk shocks, defaults of selected vulnerable counterparties and valuation adjustments contributed to a £19 billion reduction in capital, reflecting the sudden and severe shocks to financial markets.

target: loan margins recovered

metadata.original_sentence: Over recent years, as interest rates increased from historically low levels, loan margins recovered to be in line with historical averages (Chart 5.3).

source: higher expected credit losses on new loans
target: spreads on lending to UK households and businesses increase

metadata.original_sentence: First, it is assumed that spreads on lending to UK households and businesses increase by only a limited amount that reflects higher expected credit losses on new loans.

target: customers would move deposits from accounts that pay no interest to deposit accounts that do pay interest

metadata.original_sentence: Second, as interest rates increase many customers would move deposits from accounts that pay no interest to deposit accounts that do pay interest.

target: pass through around 70% of the increase in Bank Rate to sterling deposit rates

metadata.original_sentence: Third, it is assumed that banks pass through around 70% of the increase in Bank Rate to sterling deposit rates within the first year of the stress.

source: 10 percentage point change in the deposit pass-through rate
target: affect the aggregate CET1 capital ratio

metadata.original_sentence: Sensitivity analysis suggests that a 10 percentage point change in the deposit pass-through rate on sterling deposits would be expected to affect the aggregate CET1 capital ratio by around 15 basis points at the low point of the stress in year 1 (Chart 5.4).

source: exposures become riskier during the stress
target: average risk weights for credit exposures increase

metadata.original_sentence: In the first year of the stress, average risk weights for credit exposures increase from 33% to 37%, as these exposures become riskier during the stress.

target: downward pressure on CET1 capital ratios

metadata.original_sentence: The increase in risk-weights puts downward pressure on CET1 capital ratios – defined as CET1 capital as a percentage of RWAs – and they fall as RWAs increase.

target: CET1 capital ratios ... fall

metadata.original_sentence: The increase in risk-weights puts downward pressure on CET1 capital ratios – defined as CET1 capital as a percentage of RWAs – and they fall as RWAs increase.

target: rise in staff and non-staff costs

metadata.original_sentence: Expenses increase materially in later years, as the effect of higher consumer prices feeds through to a rise in staff and non-staff costs.

target: support the aggregate CET1 capital ratio

metadata.original_sentence: In the 2025 stress test, SMAs support the aggregate CET1 capital ratio by 0.8 percentage points and the aggregate leverage ratio by 0.3 percentage points.

target: support ... the aggregate leverage ratio

metadata.original_sentence: In the 2025 stress test, SMAs support the aggregate CET1 capital ratio by 0.8 percentage points and the aggregate leverage ratio by 0.3 percentage points.

source: earlier recognition of losses under IFRS 9
target: enhances transparency and market confidence

metadata.original_sentence: The earlier recognition of losses under IFRS 9 enhances transparency and market confidence in measures of banks’ capital positions, including in a downturn, thereby supporting financial stability and the safety and soundness of individual banks.

source: earlier recognition of losses under IFRS 9
target: supporting financial stability and the safety and soundness of individual banks

metadata.original_sentence: The earlier recognition of losses under IFRS 9 enhances transparency and market confidence in measures of banks’ capital positions, including in a downturn, thereby supporting financial stability and the safety and soundness of individual banks.

source: capital is drawn down earlier to raise provisions
target: bank capital, as measured under IFRS 9, is likely to fall more sharply

metadata.original_sentence: All else equal, bank capital, as measured under IFRS 9, is likely to fall more sharply in the early part of a stress as capital is drawn down earlier to raise provisions.

target: some credit losses being recognised later in the exercise

metadata.original_sentence: This has resulted in a scenario that is more closely aligned to the average of historical advanced-economy stresses and the GFC and has led to some credit losses being recognised later in the exercise.

target: a reduction in the aggregate proportion of five-year losses in the first year of the stress scenario

metadata.original_sentence: This change has contributed to a reduction in the aggregate proportion of five-year losses in the first year of the stress scenario from 59% in the 2022/23 Annual Cyclical Scenario to 52% in the 2025 Bank Capital Stress Test.

target: the start-to-trough changes in some variables being somewhat smaller

metadata.original_sentence: This updated methodology has resulted in the start-to-trough changes in some variables being somewhat smaller over the scenario horizon than they otherwise would have been.

target: reduce the five-year impairment rate on UK unsecured personal credit portfolios

metadata.original_sentence: The removal of past judgements is estimated to reduce the five-year impairment rate on UK unsecured personal credit portfolios in the 2025 Bank Capital Stress Test by 4.6 percentage points.

source: other capital buffers, such as the CCyB and CCoB
target: absorb the losses

metadata.original_sentence: For some banks, this is because other capital buffers, such as the CCyB and CCoB, would be sufficient to absorb the losses in the scenario.

source: asset values fall below agreed thresholds
target: funds may need to post additional collateral or repay part of the loan

metadata.original_sentence: If asset values fall below agreed thresholds, funds may need to post additional collateral or repay part of the loan to maintain compliance with covenants of the loan agreement.

source: high levels of debt on the underlying corporates’ balance sheets
target: This lending tends to be riskier

metadata.original_sentence: This lending tends to be riskier than other types of lending to businesses due to high levels of debt on the underlying corporates’ balance sheets.

source: capital commitments from large institutional investors and diverse asset pools with low loan to value ratios
target: historically low loss rates on private market fund financing lines

metadata.original_sentence: Loss rates on private market fund financing lines have historically been low… Banks have explained historically low loss rates on private market fund financing lines to be supported by capital commitments from large institutional investors and diverse asset pools with low loan to value ratios.

source: large repayments of the Term Funding scheme with additional incentives for Small and Medium-sized enterprises (TFSME)
target: elevated repo rates

metadata.original_sentence: The spread between gilt repo rates and Bank Rate has consistently been around its recent average, with the exception of expected periods of elevated repo rates such as period end, or following large repayments of the Term Funding scheme with additional incentives for Small and Medium-sized enterprises (TFSME).

target: improve their funding ratios

metadata.original_sentence: In contrast, firms such as insurers and pension funds, who had the highest demand for gilts historically, especially at longer maturities, have become less active as higher nominal yields improve their funding ratios, and an ageing population may encourage funds to shift toward shorter-term assets to ensure liquidity for paying current benefits.

target: encourage funds to shift toward shorter-term assets

metadata.original_sentence: In contrast, firms such as insurers and pension funds, who had the highest demand for gilts historically, especially at longer maturities, have become less active as higher nominal yields improve their funding ratios, and an ageing population may encourage funds to shift toward shorter-term assets to ensure liquidity for paying current benefits.

source: warehousing risk and intermediating between different types of market participants
target: improving market liquidity and price discovery

metadata.original_sentence: Leveraged market participants can often serve a useful function by warehousing risk and intermediating between different types of market participants, thereby improving market liquidity and price discovery.

source: The small number of funds running crowded and heavily leveraged trades in the gilt repo market
target: increases the potential risk of sharp moves

metadata.original_sentence: The small number of funds running crowded and heavily leveraged trades in the gilt repo market increases the potential risk of sharp moves as funds could need simultaneously to deleverage in response to a shock.

target: amplifying initial moves

metadata.original_sentence: The July 2025 Financial Stability Report outlined four common triggers that could lead to forced or widespread deleveraging of leveraged trades.[29] Forced or widespread deleveraging would have the result of amplifying initial moves and potentially triggering a feedback loop of further forced selling, especially if funding conditions tightened to the extent that refinancing became unavailable or prohibitively expensive.

target: triggering a feedback loop of further forced selling

metadata.original_sentence: The July 2025 Financial Stability Report outlined four common triggers that could lead to forced or widespread deleveraging of leveraged trades.[29] Forced or widespread deleveraging would have the result of amplifying initial moves and potentially triggering a feedback loop of further forced selling, especially if funding conditions tightened to the extent that refinancing became unavailable or prohibitively expensive.

source: Banks’ potential unwillingness to roll repo financing
target: counterparty concerns or balance sheet constraints

metadata.original_sentence: Banks’ potential unwillingness to roll repo financing due to counterparty concerns or balance sheet constraints, was a key finding of last year’s SWES.

target: defaults of First Brands and Tricolor

metadata.original_sentence: Markets have broadly seen the defaults of First Brands and Tricolor as driven by company-specific challenges, including alleged fraud, with no material repricing in credit markets (Section 2).

target: significant alleged fraud to go undetected

metadata.original_sentence: The opacity and complex structures may have been a contributing factor that allowed significant alleged fraud to go undetected for a period.

target: funds increasingly using other strategies to provide returns to investors

metadata.original_sentence: This has led to funds increasingly using other strategies to provide returns to investors, such as continuation vehicles that transfer ownership of selected portfolio companies to a new fund.

target: amplify stress across the financial system

source: increased geopolitical tensions and advances in technology
target: increase in risk

metadata.original_sentence: This increase in risk reflects increased geopolitical tensions and advances in technology.

target: reduce operational risks

metadata.original_sentence: New technologies such as AI and Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) provide benefits to some firms, and increased automation may reduce operational risks.

target: losses

metadata.original_sentence: This can include losses sustained due to business interruption, as well as the costs incurred from responding to and recovering from an attack or incident.

source: responding to and recovering from an attack or incident
target: costs incurred

metadata.original_sentence: This can include losses sustained due to business interruption, as well as the costs incurred from responding to and recovering from an attack or incident.

target: insurance may be unaffordable

metadata.original_sentence: For example, a 2025 DSIT report found that for UK small and medium businesses, insurance may be unaffordable, especially for firms with limited security budgets.

source: the economy adjusts to these new forms of money
target: reduced lending to businesses and households

metadata.original_sentence: The FPC welcomes this work, recognising both the transformative potential of technology associated with stablecoins and that there is a risk of reduced lending to businesses and households as the economy adjusts to these new forms of money.

target: increase corporate credit risks

metadata.original_sentence: Higher carbon prices also increase corporate credit risks due to higher costs and lower expected revenues for businesses.

source: higher costs and lower expected revenues
target: corporate credit risks

metadata.original_sentence: Higher carbon prices also increase corporate credit risks due to higher costs and lower expected revenues for businesses.

source: increased frequency and severity of extreme weather events
target: losses

metadata.original_sentence: In the physical risk-focused scenario, losses are driven by the increased frequency and severity of extreme weather events as global temperatures rise, which would have a severe negative impact on GDP and corporate earnings.

target: increased frequency and severity of extreme weather events

metadata.original_sentence: In the physical risk-focused scenario, losses are driven by the increased frequency and severity of extreme weather events as global temperatures rise, which would have a severe negative impact on GDP and corporate earnings.

source: a severe macro shock associated with transition risks
target: a 16% fall in government bond prices

metadata.original_sentence: For example, a severe macro shock associated with transition risks could lead to a 16% fall in government bond prices.

target: a smaller fall in government bond prices of around [37] 8%

metadata.original_sentence: The physical risk-focused scenario leads to a smaller fall in government bond prices of around [37] 8%, largely because there is limited interest rate risk in the scenario used – even though the negative GDP impact of the physical risk-focused scenario is much larger than in the transition risk- focused scenario.

target: Corporate bond and equity prices could also fall

metadata.original_sentence: Corporate bond and equity prices could also fall as these scenarios are priced in, with UK corporate bond prices falling by 12% and the FTSE All-Share index falling in value by around 24% under the transition risk scenario.

target: UK corporate bond prices falling by 12% and the FTSE All-Share index falling in value by around 24%

metadata.original_sentence: Corporate bond and equity prices could also fall as these scenarios are priced in, with UK corporate bond prices falling by 12% and the FTSE All-Share index falling in value by around 24% under the transition risk scenario.

source: investors price in lower cash flows and higher costs related to transition policies
target: corporate bonds issued by firms in the transport and mining and quarrying sectors could fall in value by as much as 60%

metadata.original_sentence: For example, corporate bonds issued by firms in the transport and mining and quarrying sectors could fall in value by as much as 60% as investors price in lower cash flows and higher costs related to transition policies.

source: downgraded from investment grade to high yield
target: required to sell corporate bonds

metadata.original_sentence: For example, institutional investors could be required to sell corporate bonds if they were downgraded from investment grade to high yield or be forced to sell assets to meet liquidity needs from redemptions or margin calls on leveraged exposures.

source: redemptions or margin calls on leveraged exposures
target: forced to sell assets

metadata.original_sentence: For example, institutional investors could be required to sell corporate bonds if they were downgraded from investment grade to high yield or be forced to sell assets to meet liquidity needs from redemptions or margin calls on leveraged exposures.

target: increase the corporate bond price impact

metadata.original_sentence: Such actions could increase the corporate bond price impact of the transition-focused scenario by around 4 percentage points.

target: increases in carbon prices

metadata.original_sentence: These businesses are vulnerable to increases in carbon prices, which can occur as a result of government policy, but also in response to increases in gas prices.

target: increases in carbon prices

metadata.original_sentence: These businesses are vulnerable to increases in carbon prices, which can occur as a result of government policy, but also in response to increases in gas prices.

source: EPC rating of ‘C’ or above could become a minimum requirement for rentability
target: losses on their lending secured on buy-to-let properties

metadata.original_sentence: Banks could face losses on their lending secured on buy-to-let properties as an EPC rating of ‘C’ or above could become a minimum requirement for rentability as early as 2028.

target: Losses to banks from flooding are also mitigated

metadata.original_sentence: Losses to banks from flooding are also mitigated by the high availability of flood insurance, including due to the presence of Flood Re (more information below).

source: FPC’s loan to income (LTI) flow limit
target: protection against the UK household sector becoming overly indebted

metadata.original_sentence: Most UK mortgages have an LTV ratio below 70%, and the FPC’s loan to income (LTI) flow limit provides protection against the UK household sector becoming overly indebted.

source: strong flood insurance coverage and affordability
target: financial impact of flooding on households is likely to be limited

metadata.original_sentence: As set out in the November 2024 Financial Stability Report, the shorter-term financial impact of flooding on households is likely to be limited due to strong flood insurance coverage and affordability relative to other countries.

target: Credit risks to banks from physical hazards are also currently mitigated

metadata.original_sentence: This is in part due to Flood Re – a joint reinsurance initiative between the Government and the insurance industry, which promotes the affordability and availability of insurance for households at high risk of flooding.[39] Credit risks to banks from physical hazards are also currently mitigated by high insurance coverage.

target: decreases in insurance coverage

metadata.original_sentence: Taken together, these changes could lead to decreases in insurance coverage due to insurers withdrawing from certain regions, or pricing becoming unaffordable for households.

target: decreases in insurance coverage

metadata.original_sentence: Taken together, these changes could lead to decreases in insurance coverage due to insurers withdrawing from certain regions, or pricing becoming unaffordable for households.

source: uninsurability and the costs of repair from physical damages
target: lower house prices

metadata.original_sentence: This could pose financial stability risks in the long term by negatively affecting: Households – through lower house prices due to uninsurability and the costs of repair from physical damages as well as difficulty remortgaging a property if there is a lack of available insurance.

target: higher losses on lending to households and businesses

metadata.original_sentence: Banks – through higher losses on lending to households and businesses affected by flooding.

source: Investment in physical resilience and actions to enhance financial resilience
target: UK financial stability

metadata.original_sentence: Investment in physical resilience and actions to enhance financial resilience could be beneficial to UK financial stability by supporting insurability as physical risks increase.

target: insurability

metadata.original_sentence: Investment in physical resilience and actions to enhance financial resilience could be beneficial to UK financial stability by supporting insurability as physical risks increase.

source: targeted enhancement of the UK bank resolution regime
target: regime remains fit for purpose and ready for use

metadata.original_sentence: The FPC supported this targeted enhancement of the UK bank resolution regime which had helped to ensure the regime remains fit for purpose and ready for use.

target: reissued the O-SII buffer rates

metadata.original_sentence: As a result, the FPC reissued the O-SII buffer rates for 2024 to reflect this updated framework.

target: deep recessions across global economies

metadata.original_sentence: Annex 2: 2025 Bank Capital Stress Test: bank-specific results The 2025 Bank Capital Stress Test assessed the resilience of the major UK banks and building societies against a severe negative global supply disruption that led to deep recessions across global economies.

source: higher interest rates, inflation, unemployment and house price falls
target: UK macroeconomic stress

metadata.original_sentence: UK-focused banks – Lloyds Banking Group, Nationwide Building Society, NatWest Group and Santander UK Group Holdings plc were most affected by the UK macroeconomic stress, driven by higher interest rates, inflation, unemployment and house price falls.

target: losses for banks

metadata.original_sentence: The results incorporate losses against the following key risk types: Credit impairments were the main source of losses for banks.

target: penalised banks

metadata.original_sentence: The corset was a mechanism that penalised banks for rapidly expanding their deposits, thus constraining the rate at which banks could increase their domestic lending.

target: constraining the rate at which banks could increase their domestic lending

metadata.original_sentence: The corset was a mechanism that penalised banks for rapidly expanding their deposits, thus constraining the rate at which banks could increase their domestic lending.

target: increase impairments

metadata.original_sentence: The higher cost of living, which is particularly pronounced on vulnerable households, is estimated to increase impairments relative to the scenario starting point by 4% for mortgages and 20% for consumer credit.

target: households are more resilient

metadata.original_sentence: This is an improvement relative to the 2022/23 ACS, which reflects that households are more resilient due to increased real incomes as wage growth has outpaced inflation since the 2022/23 ACS and a lower inflation peak in this year’s test.

source: PRC implemented proposals to reduce minimum capital requirements
target: overall loss- absorbing capacity in the banking system broadly unchanged

metadata.original_sentence: In 2019 the FPC raised the rate at which it expects to set the UK CCyB in a standard risk environment from 1% to 2%, alongside which the PRC implemented proposals to reduce minimum capital requirements in a way that left overall loss- absorbing capacity in the banking system broadly unchanged.

target: change in the PRC’s risk tolerance

metadata.original_sentence: This was intentionally not reflected in the stress-testing framework at the time as to do so would have resulted in a change in the PRC’s risk tolerance.

source: cooling failure in a data centre
target: outage across a number of its currency and futures markets

metadata.original_sentence: On 28 November 2025, CME group suffered an outage across a number of its currency and futures markets, reported to have been linked to a cooling failure in a data centre.

target: bond prices

metadata.original_sentence: But the effect of lower risk premia on bond prices is significantly outweighed by the increased duration risk.

target: bond prices
